content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Manage My CalNet Account

  - url: https://calnet.berkeley.edu/calnet-me/manage-my-calnet-account

  - source: UC Berkeley CalNet Identity and Access Management

  - domain: calnet.berkeley.edu


  ## Content


  # Manage My CalNet Account


  CalNet Account Manager at mycalnet.berkeley.edu provides self-service account-management
  functions for students, employees, affiliates, alumni, and student delegates. Available
  functions include changing a known passphrase, updating a recovery email address,
  managing 2-Step settings, and using recovery tools for a forgotten CalNet ID or
  passphrase.


  Self-service recovery depends on information already associated with the account.
  A recovery email address is required for the self-service tools used to recover
  a forgotten CalNet ID or passphrase. Users who can still sign in can add or update
  that recovery address from CalNet Account Manager.


  ## When the user cannot sign in


  A user who cannot sign in and needs to add or correct a recovery email address may
  contact CalNet support. The request should identify the account using the applicable
  student, alumni, UCPath, or CalNet identifier. CalNet requires additional identity
  verification before processing such a request.


  For a forgotten CalNet ID or passphrase, the user can begin at mycalnet.berkeley.edu
  and choose the forgotten-ID or passphrase option. The recovery process asks the
  user to select an identifier type, provide the corresponding identifier or recovery
  email address, complete the automated check, and request reset instructions. If
  a recovery email address is present, reset instructions are sent there.


  If the self-service process is unsuccessful, CalNet support can assist. The official
  page states that identity may need to be verified through a remote Zoom session.
  The support route provides an official way to resolve an account-recovery request
  when the self-service process cannot be completed.


  ## Managing 2-Step


  CalNet Account Manager also links to the device-management information for CalNet
  2-Step. Device enrollment and the completion of a 2-Step prompt follow the separate
  2-Step process.


  The page treats passphrase recovery, recovery-email maintenance, and 2-Step device
  management as separate account-management functions and directs users to the applicable
  instructions for each function.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Manage My CalNet Account
url: https://calnet.berkeley.edu/calnet-me/manage-my-calnet-account

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Fake Email Account Suspension Email

  - url: https://security.berkeley.edu/news/fake-email-account-suspension-email

  - source: UC Berkeley Information Security Office

  - domain: security.berkeley.edu


  ## Content


  # Fake Account-Suspension Messages and Credential Safety


  UC Berkeley''s Information Security Office describes fraudulent messages that claim
  a campus email or account will be suspended unless the recipient acts immediately.
  These messages use urgent language and may imitate a campus technician or official
  service.


  ## Credential boundary


  UC Berkeley Help Desks do not initiate account-support contact by sending unsolicited
  text messages to personal phone numbers. A technician will not ask a user to send
  a password, a Duo push code, or other secret account information through email,
  text, chat, or another insecure channel.


  Campus CalNet credentials should be entered only on a UC Berkeley CalNet CAS authentication
  page. A Google Form, Microsoft Office form, general support form, or other non-CAS
  page is not an acceptable place to enter a CalNet password or authentication secret.


  These restrictions apply even when the message claims that immediate verification
  is required to preserve access to a university service. The need to complete verification
  does not make an authentication secret an ordinary support field. A support worker
  or intermediary cannot establish the user''s authenticated CalNet session by receiving
  the user''s password, Duo approval code, passcode, or similar secret.


  ## What to do with an unexpected request


  If a request was not expected, reject it or verify it separately using known campus
  contact information. Do not rely on the contact details or link contained in the
  suspicious message. Messages received through campus email can be reported using
  the phishing-report function. Suspicious text messages can be captured and forwarded
  to the campus phishing-report address.


  The purpose of the guidance is to keep account authentication between the account
  holder and the official campus authentication service. Support may help diagnose
  an account problem, explain recovery options, or verify identity through an approved
  process, but users should not transmit authentication credentials or approve a login
  request for another person.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Fake Email Account Suspension Email
url: https://security.berkeley.edu/news/fake-email-account-suspension-email

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: CalNet 2-Step

  - url: https://calnet.berkeley.edu/calnet-2-step

  - source: UC Berkeley CalNet Identity and Access Management

  - domain: calnet.berkeley.edu


  ## Content


  # CalNet 2-Step Verification


  CalNet 2-Step is UC Berkeley''s multi-factor authentication process. It protects
  a CalNet account by requiring both the normal CalNet sign-in and a second verification
  step. Students and other covered CalNet users are required to use 2-Step for protected
  campus services.


  ## How the login is completed


  1. The account holder enrolls one or more verification devices.

  2. The account holder signs in with the CalNet ID and passphrase.

  3. When the 2-Step prompt appears, the account holder uses an enrolled device to
  complete the second step.

  4. Depending on the enrolled method, the user may approve a push notification or
  enter a passcode in the official prompt.


  The second step reconfirms the identity of the person signing in by using a verification
  device enrolled for the CalNet account.


  Bypass codes are available as a backup method when a verification device is unavailable
  or when cellular or Wi-Fi service is limited. The page recommends generating and
  printing bypass codes after device registration.


  ## Support and device problems


  UC Berkeley provides 2-Step support through IT Client Services, including a dedicated
  support email address. The page also links to instructions for registering and using
  devices, saving bypass codes, recommended practices, travel, and frequently asked
  questions.

  '
metadata: {}
title: CalNet 2-Step
url: https://calnet.berkeley.edu/calnet-2-step

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Payment & Refunds - Cal Student Central

  - url: https://studentcentral.berkeley.edu/billing-fees-payments/payment-refunds/

  - source: UC Berkeley Cal Student Central

  - domain: studentcentral.berkeley.edu


  ## Content


  # Payments, Student Accounts, and Refunds


  UC Berkeley bills tuition and registration fees through CalCentral. Students can
  use the My Finances area and the Billing Summary transaction view for current charges,
  adjustments, financial-aid disbursements, payments, and student-account status.
  Monthly statements may not show the most recent activity, so the transaction view
  is the current account source.


  ## Refund timing and account status


  Financial aid and related refunds may begin to disburse before instruction starts
  when the student''s financial-aid file is complete and required tasks have been
  satisfied. The actual timing depends on when aid is applied to the student''s CalCentral
  account and whether holds or incomplete tasks remain.


  A refund generally results when credits on the student account exceed the charges
  on that account. Outside scholarships, departmental awards, payments, and changes
  to the bill can affect the amount. A student who sees a difference between expected
  aid, the bill, and the refund should review the account in CalCentral and contact
  Cal Student Central when necessary.


  Refunds are issued through the student''s CalCentral account, except for specified
  Parent PLUS Loan handling. The two general delivery methods are direct deposit to
  a U.S. bank account or a mailed check to the local address recorded for the student.
  Refund checks are not picked up in person.


  ## Direct deposit and account information


  Direct deposit is the faster delivery method once a refund is available. Bank-account
  information entered for student-refund direct deposit is used for refunds and is
  not used by the university to make payments on the student''s behalf.


  The page directs students to CalCentral for the most current account, aid-disbursement,
  payment, and refund information. It also provides Cal Student Central as the support
  route when the bill, expected aid, and refund do not match.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Payment & Refunds - Cal Student Central
url: https://studentcentral.berkeley.edu/billing-fees-payments/payment-refunds/

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Student Records - Cal Student Central

  - url: https://studentcentral.berkeley.edu/student-information-access/student-records/

  - source: UC Berkeley Cal Student Central

  - domain: studentcentral.berkeley.edu


  ## Content


  # Student Records and CalCentral Access


  CalCentral is UC Berkeley''s personalized student dashboard. It brings together
  student information from multiple campus systems and is the place where students
  review messages and take action on billing, financial aid, enrollment, grades, records,
  and advising.


  Students use CalNet authentication to sign in to CalCentral. Students access the
  personalized dashboard through CalNet authentication. CalCentral then presents the
  student data and actions available through the connected campus systems.


  ## Common student-record functions


  Students can use CalCentral to:


  - review student billing and financial-aid information;

  - view enrollment, grades, academic records, and advising information;

  - read campus messages and task notifications;

  - access the student profile and assigned student ID;

  - update mailing address, phone number, and email information;

  - manage selected information-disclosure settings.


  A student''s ten-digit student ID can be found from the CalCentral profile by selecting
  the student''s name and opening the Profile page. Mailing and contact details are
  also managed from the Profile area. University communications and mailed refund
  checks use the address recorded in CalCentral, subject to the applicable student-account
  process.


  ## Access and support


  A person who needs limited access to a student''s information can use the university''s
  delegate-access process. The student grants the applicable permissions from the
  student account.


  For a CalCentral functionality problem, Student Information Systems provides support.
  The page directs users with CalCentral functionality problems to Student Information
  Systems and links to CalCentral support resources.


  Student Information Systems operates academic and administrative processes used
  by departments for financial aid, registration, enrollment, advising, billing, payments,
  and records. '
